sanskrit_devanagari:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् ।
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रता: ॥ २८ ॥
sanskrit_iast: yeṣāṁ tv anta-gataṁ pāpaṁjanānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇāmte dvandva-moha-nirmuktābhajante māṁ dṛḍha-vratāḥ

translations.verbatim_quote: Persons who have acted piously in previous lives and in this life and whose sinful actions are completely eradicated are freed from the dualities of delusion, and they engage themselves in My service with determination.

Persons who have acted piously in previous lives and in this life and whose sinful actions are completely eradicated are freed from the dualities of delusion, and they engage themselves in My service with determination.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 1557 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Those eligible for elevation to the transcendental position are mentioned in this verse. For those who are sinful, atheistic, foolish and deceitful, it is very difficult to transcend the duality of desire and hate.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.28 Yesam jananam, those persons; tu, on the other hand; punya-karmanam, who are of virtuous deeds, in whom exist virtuous deeds that are the cause of purification of the mind; whose papam, sin; antagatam, has come to an end, is almost eradicated, attenuated; te, they; dvandva-moha-nirmuk
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-28-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Those persons (yeṣām … janānām) of meritorious deeds (puṇya-karmaṇām, in whom the deeds purify the mind), whose sin (pāpam) has come to an end (anta-gatam, almost eradicated, attenuated) — they, being free from the delusion of duality named in 7.27 (dvandva-moha-nirmuktā) and firm in their convictions (dṛḍha-vratāḥ), worship Krishna.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.28 However, there are some whose sins, accruing from beginningless time, which cause desire or hatred to the pairs of opposites and annual the tendency towards Me, have come to an end, i.e., have become weakened, through the accumulation of good actions in numerous births, They resort to
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-28-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Those whose sins, accruing from beginningless time, which cause desire/hatred toward pairs of opposites and annul the tendency toward Krishna, have weakened through the accumulation of good actions in many births — they resort to Krishna, devoid of guṇa-produced delusion, and worship in proportion to the excellence of their previous Karma.

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All readings agree on the structural arc: virtuous-deed accumulation → end of sin → freedom from dvandva-moha → firm-vowed worship. Shankara reads it through mind-purification (puṇya-karma purifies the antaḥkaraṇa, dissolving the duality-trap of 7.27); Ramanuja reads it through cross-life karma-accumulation (the obstructions to Krishna-orientation weaken). The engineering analog at career-scope is STRONG: the senior whose track record of right-action has dissolved the cycle of self-recrimination/self-praise.

literal_meaning: Those of meritorious deeds whose sin has ended — they, freed from delusion of dualities, worship me with firm vows.
traditional_meaning_consensus: yeṣāṁ tv anta-gataṁ pāpaṁ janānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇām / te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṁ dṛḍha-vratāḥ — those persons of meritorious deeds (puṇya-karmaṇām) whose sin has ended (anta-gataṁ pāpam), they (te), freed from the delusion of dualities (dvandva-moha-nirmuktā), worship Krishna with firm vows (dṛḍha-vratāḥ). Shankara: those whose sin has come to an end through virtuous deeds (which purify the mind), being free from the delusion of dualities described in 7.27 and firm in their convictions, worship Krishna. Ramanuja: those whose sins (which cause desire-aversion to the pairs of opposites and obstruct the tendency toward Krishna) have weakened through accumulated good actions across many births resort to Krishna and worship in proportion to the excellence of their previous Karma.

remaining_gaps: Madhva, Abhinavagupta, Sridhara Swami, Kesava Kashmiri commentaries available in raw/ but not pulled into the source pack.
